channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Breakthrough tech can no longer rely on CPUs
item.description: In 1993, Intel released the first Pentium processor. By today’s standards, it was primitive. At the time, it represented a major—and exciting—leap forward, even if it didn’t quite match the Macintosh’s Motorola 601 chip in sheer number-crunching performance.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Aramburu, Fast Company

item.title: Better together? Why AWS is unifying data analytics and AI services in SageMaker
item.description: Data warehousing, business intelligence, data analytics, and AI services are all coming together under one roof at Amazon Web Services.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get ready to build your own Lego masterpieces with this new tech
item.description: Brick My World is an innovative app that allows you to turn any object into a Lego creation with your phone using advanced artificial intelligence and mobile scanning technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bitcoin $100K party: Why the price is a psychological milestone for longtime crypto believers
item.description: Crypto enthusiasts are rallying on social media over their new riches after Bitcoin crossed the $100,000 threshold on Thursday. “$100,000 is a psychological milestone,” entrepreneur and investor Anthony Pompliano wrote on X.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Telework, IT modernization emerge as early DOGE targets
item.description: The plans for DOGE continue to evolve, but some early themes are emerging as lawmakers start to weigh in with their own ideas. The post Telework, IT modernization emerge as early DOGE targets first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Apex the $45M stegosaurus is on display in New York. Here's what scientists hope to learn about it
item.description: The most expensive dinosaur fossil ever discovered will be on view in New York starting this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The power of unified data storage with generative AI
item.description: The promise of generative AI (genAI) is undeniable, but the volume and complexity of the data involved pose significant challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Quake prompts brief tsunami warning on the West Coast. Here's what to know about tsunamis
item.description: The powerful earthquake that struck in Northern California on Thursday prompted a brief tsunami warning affecting about 5 million people along a stretch of the West Coast — from Northern California to Oregon — before being canceled.
item.dc:creator: Mark Thiessen, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cisco strengthens AWS integration to speed troubleshooting in multicloud environments
item.description: Cisco this week said it has bolstered integrations between its own hybrid cloud offerings and Amazon Web Services (AWS) to improve visibility and help enterprise customers more accurately troubleshoot application availability issues across multicloud networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google DeepMind and World Labs unveil AI tools to create 3D spaces from simple prompts
item.description: Google DeepMind and startup World Labs this week both revealed previews of AI tools that can be used to create immersive 3D environments from simple prompts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data- and people-driven transformation: How the Port of Auckland is navigating the future
item.description: The data and insights team at the Port of Auckland is an enterprise-level team that provides information in a systematic and consumable way to different departments in the organization so they can make data-driven decisions, see how they’re progressing toward strategic outcomes and base their decisi
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to bulk delete spam and old text messages on Android
item.description: If unwanted text messages are piling up in your Android inbox, there are some easy steps you can take to clean things up and keep them organized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese online retailer Temu suspended in Vietnam
item.description: HANOI, Vietnam (AP) — Vietnam has suspended the operations of Chinese online retailer Temu after it failed to meet a government deadline to register the company by the end of November.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The largest artificial intelligence data center ever built by Facebook’s parent company Meta is coming to northeast Louisiana, the company said Wednesday, bringing hopes that the $10 billion facility will transform an economically neglected corner of the state. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House says at least 8 US telecom firms, dozens of nations impacted by China hacking campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top White House official on Wednesday said at least eight U.S. telecom firms and dozens of nations have been impacted by a Chinese hacking campaign.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Patreon Class Action Settlement: What You Need to Know
item.description: Patreon users may be eligible for payment from a new class action settlement alleging disclosure of subscribers' information to Meta.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman 'not that worried' about rival Elon Musk's influence in the Trump administration
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, who is in a legal dispute with rival Elon Musk, said he is “not that worried” about Musk's influence in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OECD: GenAI is affecting jobs previously thought safe from automation
item.description: According to a new report from the Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD), generative AI (genAI) will soon affect work areas previously considered to have a low likelihood of automation, according to The Register.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI catches cancer that mammogram misses
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The FBI now says encryption is good for you
item.description: Apple has faced an unequal battle in recent years as some lawmakers, the FBI, and regulators insist that the company create backdoors through which to access messages and other parts of its platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Max to begin cracking down on password sharing next week
item.description: Max will begin its password-sharing crackdown next week, beginning with "very early, gentle messaging" directed at high-usage users.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Intel’s CEO, Pat Gelsinger, ‘retires’ — riiiiight
item.description: Back in the 1990s, when Bill Clinton was president, people figured out they might just want to be on the internet, and Intel and Microsoft were the major technology powers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft moves to stop M365 Copilot from ‘oversharing’ data
item.description: As Microsoft pushes for businesses to adopt its Microsoft 365 Copilot, many customers have run into a major stumbling block: the AI assistant’s ability to surface confidential information to employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee to go to jurors
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments in the high-profile murder trial of a tech consultant charged in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee concluded Tuesday, with both sides urging jurors to use their common sense to deliver justice.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI weapons detection system used in Tennessee schools sued by FTC
item.description: The technology scans students and other visitors for weapons, using AI, without requiring them to remove items from their person, according to previous marketing.
item.dc:creator: Tori Gessner, NewsNation

item.title: Kotlin previews guard conditions in when expressions
item.description: JetBrains has released Kotlin 2.1.0, an update to the Kotlin programming language that previews guard conditions and has compiler updates offering flexibility around checks. Released November 27, Kotlin 2.1.0 can be installed as an update by following the instructions at kotlinlang.org. Kotlin 2.1.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI
item.description: There is a need to create secure computing capabilities that maintain the privacy and confidentiality of AI models and data sets. The post Securing the AI data pipeline with confidential AI first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: If you want security, start with secure products
item.description: While in these post-CrowdStrike days it is reasonable to think one of the best ways to improve endpoint security in any business is to replace Windows with Apple devices, that’s just the starting point in a serious security journey. But it’s a pretty good starting point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025
item.description: Fiscal 2025 is shaping up to be busy for the Army National Guard as it pushes to complete migration of all end-user devices to Entra ID and Intune. The post Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network
